                Set -> lists:min(Set)

    % Start building DP table row by row

        Row = lists:nth(RowIndex, Grid),
        
        % Build the current row based on the previous rows stored in CurrentDP
        NewRow = build_row(Grid, M, N, RowIndex, CurrentDP),
        
        % Append the new row to the DP table
        lists:append(CurrentDP, [NewRow])
    end, [] , 0).

build_row(Grid, M, N, i, CurrentDP) ->

        lists:append(CurrentRow, [Unique_S_ij])
    end, [], 0).

    dp_step(0, [])
    where

solve(Grid) ->
    M = length(Grid),
    N = length(Grid[0]),
    MaxXor = 1023.

    % --- Helper functions for bit manipulation ---

    % transform(Mask, C): Calculates the new mask resulting from XORing all reachable states in Mask with C.
    transform(Mask, C) ->
        iterate(0, 0, Mask, C).

    % iterate(K, NewMask, Mask, C): Tail recursive helper for transformation.
    iterate(K, NewMask, Mask, C) when K >= 1024 ->
        NewMask;
    iterate(K, NewMask, Mask, C) ->
        Case = Mask and (1 << K),
        NewMask_next =
            if Case =\= 0 ->
                K_prime = K xor C,
                NewMask or (1 << K_prime)
            ;
                NewMask
            end,
        iterate(K + 1, NewMask_next, Mask, C).

    % --- DP Initialization: Calculate Row 0 ---

    % row0_masks will store DP[0][j] for j=0 to N-1
    Row0 = calculate_row0(N, Grid[0], N).

    calculate_row0(0, _, _) ->
        [] ;
    calculate_row0(1, Grid0, N) ->
        [1 << Grid0[0]] ;
    calculate_row0(J, Grid0, N) ->
        PrevMask = lists:last(calculate_row0(J-1, Grid0, N)),
        CurrentMask = transform(PrevMask, Grid0[J]),
        [calculate_row0(J-1, Grid0, N) | CurrentMask].

    % Since building the list iteratively in Erlang functional style is verbose, 
    % we use a simpler iterative approach for Row0 construction based on the list structure:
    calculate_row0_simple(N, Grid0) ->
        [1 << Grid0[0]] =:= 0 -> % Handle N=0 case defensively, though constraints guarantee N>=1
        if N =:= 1 ->
            [1 << Grid0[0]]
        else
            lists:foldl(
                fun(RowAcc, J) ->
                    PrevMask = lists:last(RowAcc),
                    CurrentMask = transform(PrevMask, Grid0[J]),
                    RowAcc ++ [CurrentMask]
                end,
                [1 << Grid0[0]],
                lists:seq(1, N-1)
            )
        end.


    % --- DP Iteration: Calculate subsequent rows ---

    % dp_solver(i, prev_row): Recursively calculates rows i, i+1, ..., M-1
    dp_solver(M, _ ) ->
        % Base case: Finished all rows.
        % The result is the last row calculated.
        error(no_result_found) % Should not happen if M>=1
    dp_solver(I, PrevRow) when I >= M ->
        PrevRow;
    dp_solver(I, PrevRow) ->
        % Calculate CurrentRow[I] based on PrevRow[I-1] and CurrentRow[I-1] (which is being built)
        CurrentRow = calculate_row_i(N, Grid[I], PrevRow),
        dp_solver(I + 1, CurrentRow).

    % calculate_row_i(N, Grid_I, PrevRow): Builds row I
    calculate_row_i(N, GridI, PrevRow) ->
        [TopMask, LeftMask] = [0, 0], % Placeholder initialization for the first element
        % Calculate the first element R_i[0]
        R_i0 = transform(PrevRow[0], GridI[0]),
        
        % Use foldl to build the rest of the row iteratively
        lists:foldl(
            fun(RowAcc, J) ->
                % J is the current column index (starting from 1)
                G_ij = GridI[J],
                
                % Contribution from Top (i-1, j)
                T_mask = transform(PrevRow[J], G_ij),
                
                % Contribution from Left (i, j-1)
                L_mask = transform(lists:last(RowAcc), G_ij),
                
                % New mask is the OR combination
                NewMask = T_mask or L_mask,
                
                RowAcc ++ [NewMask]
            end,
            [R_i0], % Initial accumulator is the first element
            lists:seq(1, N-1)
        ).

    % --- Execution ---

    % 1. Calculate Row 0
    R0 = calculate_row0_simple(N, Grid[0]).

    % 2. Solve iteratively from Row 1 to M-1
    FinalRow = dp_solver(1, R0).

    % 3. Extract the result
    FinalMask = lists:last(FinalRow).
    
    % Find the minimum k such that the k-th bit is set in FinalMask
    min_k = 1023,
    find_min_k(min_k, FinalMask).

    find_min_k(K, Mask) when K < 0 ->
        0; % Should not happen if M*N >= 1
    find_min_k(K, Mask) ->
        Case = Mask and (1 << K),
        if Case =\= 0 ->
            K % Found the smallest K
        else
            find_min_k(K - 1, Mask) % Check smaller values
        end.
